nodes.summary: `update.sh` is a deployment automation script that orchestrates a full end-to-end update of the **shame.skogai.se** web property. It handles source code synchronization from the remote Git repository and deploys both the static frontend (Cloudflare Pages) and the serverless backend (Cloudflare Worker) in a single, sequ…

`App` is the entry point and root orchestrator of the DOTA2 HUB client application — it bootstraps the React component tree, owns all top-level state, and dispatches rendering to page-level components (`SquadPage`, `SoloPage`, `DraftPage`, `WallOfShame`, `MatchAutopsyPage`) and shared UI components (`Marquee`, `AgentTe…

`worker/src/index.js` is the entry point and sole request dispatcher for the `shame.skogai.se` Cloudflare Worker admin API — it receives all inbound HTTP requests, routes them to inline handlers, enforces Bearer-token authentication, and reads/writes JSON values to a bound Cloudflare KV namespace (`SHAME_KV`).

tour.title: Start Here: Project Overview
tour.description: Begin with the onboarding and AI guidance docs to understand the project's purpose, conventions, and where everything lives before touching any code.

tour.title: Frontend Entry Point
tour.description: Explore the main React application entry point to understand how the frontend is bootstrapped, what top-level state exists, and how components are composed together.

tour.title: Static Data Assets
tour.description: Review the static JSON data files that drive the application's content — squad rosters, match results, and shame records — to understand the core data shapes used throughout the UI.

tour.title: Data Loading Layer
tour.description: Understand how the frontend fetches and loads data at runtime, including how it falls back to static data when live sources are unavailable.

tour.title: Core UI Components
tour.description: Walk through the shared utilities and squad-specific components that form the backbone of the UI, paying attention to reusable patterns and how squad data is rendered.

tour.title: Page and Admin Components
tour.description: Examine the page-level components and admin interface to see how routing and privileged views are structured within the frontend.

tour.title: Autopsy and Tweaks UI
tour.description: Look at the autopsy component and tweaks panel to understand how post-match analysis is displayed and how runtime UI configuration is managed.

tour.title: Backend Worker Entry Point
tour.description: Dive into the Cloudflare Worker backend to understand how incoming requests are handled, routed, and responded to from the edge.

tour.title: Worker Routing Architecture
tour.description: Read the routing documentation to understand the conventions and patterns used for structuring routes within the worker layer.

tour.title: Data Transformation Tooling
tour.description: Review the OpenDota-to-match conversion guide to understand how raw external API data is transformed into the application's internal match format.

tour.title: Deployment and Update Automation
tour.description: Finish by reviewing the deployment documentation and update script to understand how the project is shipped and kept up to date in production.
